ownership.name: Green Bay Packers Inc. (publicly owned — only community-owned NFL franchise)
ownership.style: Board of Directors oversees; Mark Murphy is president/CEO; unique community ownership model
ownership.tendencies: Stability-oriented; trust front office and coaching staff; organic development approach

gm.name: Brian Gutekunst
gm.philosophy: Aggressive trader — acquired Micah Parsons from Dallas, giving up 1st-round pick + Kenny Clark; values impact players; willing to sacrifice draft capital for proven talent
gm.analytics_approach: Hybrid — Ted Thompson's traditional scouting legacy blended with modern analytics

head_coach.name: Matt LaFleur
head_coach.scheme_offense: Shanahan-tree outside zone; play-action heavy; motion and pre-snap shifts; RPOs
head_coach.scheme_defense: DC Jonathan Gannon (hired from Cardinals HC firing); multiple fronts with exotic pressures

decision_makers.primary: Brian Gutekunst (GM) with Matt LaFleur (HC) strong collaboration

roster_2026.qb_situation: Jordan Love — franchise QB; locked in as starter; team built around his development
key_departures: Kenny Clark (DT, traded to Dallas in Parsons deal)
roster_2026.cap_space: unverified — could save $20M by moving on from Elgton Jenkins
expiring_contracts: Elgton Jenkins (G/C, potential $20M cap savings if released)

traded_away: Round 1 Pick 20 (to Dallas in Micah Parsons trade)

needs.reason: Nate Hobbs experiment at outside CB didn't work; Carrington Valentine and Keisean Nixon are solid but need premier talent on perimeter
Lost Kenny Clark in Parsons trade; Devonte Wyatt returning from season-ending ankle injury; interior defensive line depleted
Elgton Jenkins could be released for $20M cap savings (age 30, fractured fibula); center replacement needed; Jordan Morgan could move to LT
